invoiceDueDays.description: Number of days until invoice is due (1-120)

enableInvoiceReminderFee.description: Enable invoice reminder fee (null = use default)

customTexts.description: Custom invoice text templates. Content can be provided as HTML or Markdown and will be converted to internal format.

url.description: Public URL to access the generated invoice file

filename.description: Filename of the generated invoice file

id.description: Unique identifier for the potential invoice. Format: {type}::{projectId}::{dateFrom}::{dateTo}. This ID is used to reference the invoice in other endpoints.

projectId.description: Project ID (UUID) associated with this potential invoice. This is the project where the billable work was performed.

orgId.description: Organization ID (UUID) of the customer organization for this potential invoice. This is the organization that will be billed.

dateKey.description: Date key used for grouping potential invoices by month (format: YYYY-MM). Used for organizing invoices in the list view.

dateFrom.description: Start date of the billing period (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is when the billable period begins.

dateTo.description: End date of the billing period (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is when the billable period ends.

total.description: Total net amount for this potential invoice (before tax). This is the sum of all billable items in the invoice.

type.description: Type of invoice indicating what kind of billable items are included. See InvoiceType enum for all possible values.

id.description: Unique identifier for the potential invoice. Format: {type}::{projectId}::{dateFrom}::{dateTo}

hasMeta.description: Whether this potential invoice has custom metadata or settings applied. If true, the invoice has been customized (custom dates, contact, skipped items, etc.). If false, it shows the default calculated values.

periodFrom.description: Actual service period start date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is when the billable work actually began.

periodTo.description: Actual service period end date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is when the billable work actually ended.

type.description: Primary invoice type indicating the main category of billable items. See InvoiceType enum for all possible values.

includedTypes.description: Array of all invoice types included in this potential invoice. For Mixed type invoices, this will contain multiple types (e.g., TimeBased, Subscription, Products).

projectId.description: Project ID (UUID) where the billable work was performed

organizationId.description: Organization ID (UUID) of the customer organization that will be billed

orgHourlyRate.description: Hourly rate (in organization currency) used for calculating time-based billing. This rate is applied to logged hours on tasks.

tax.description: Tax amount calculated for this invoice. The tax rate is configured in workspace settings.

applyReverseCharge.description: Whether reverse charge VAT is applied. Reverse charge is used for B2B transactions within the EU where the customer is responsible for VAT.

contactId.description: Contact ID (UUID) of the organization member who will receive the invoice when it is sent via email. Null if no contact has been assigned yet.

billedFrom.description: Custom billed from date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is the start date that will appear on the final invoice document. Can differ from periodFrom if you want to show a different date range. Null if using default periodFrom.

billedTo.description: Custom billed to date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). This is the end date that will appear on the final invoice document. Can differ from periodTo if you want to show a different date range. Null if using default periodTo.

leadtimeFeePercent.description: Leadtime service fee percentage. This is the percentage of the invoice total that will be charged as the Leadtime platform fee.

addLeadtimeFeeToInvoice.description: Whether to add the Leadtime service fee as a separate line item on the invoice. If false, the fee is not shown on the invoice but may still be calculated separately.

addLeadtimeFeeMethod.description: Method for how the Leadtime fee should be added to the invoice. See AddLeadtimeFeeMethod enum for options (e.g., SeparateRow, DistributedToTasks).

addLeadtimeFeeTasks.description: Array of task IDs (UUIDs) for distributing the Leadtime fee across specific tasks. Used when addLeadtimeFeeMethod is set to distribute the fee to tasks.

addLeadtimeRowCustomTitle.description: Custom title for the Leadtime fee row on the invoice. If null, a default title is used. This allows you to customize how the fee appears on the invoice document.

subscriptions.description: Array of subscription billing rows. These are recurring fees (monthly or periodic) that are due for billing. Each row includes billing type, payment type, price, quantity, and optional manual title.

expressQuotations.description: Array of accepted express quotations. Express quotations are quick quotes created directly from tickets that have been accepted by the customer. Each quotation includes items, quantities, unit prices, and acceptance details.

tasks.description: Array of time-based tasks (tickets) with logged hours. These are completed tasks that have time logged and are ready for billing. Each task includes logged time, estimated time, billed time, and billing information.

products.description: Array of products from the product catalog. These are standardized products or services with fixed prices, quantities, options, and discounts. Product descriptions are returned as HTML.

components.description: Array of project components with time estimates and hourly rates. Components are work packages from the project structure. Each component includes nested items, time frames, hourly rates, and amounts. Descriptions are returned as HTML.

manualPositions.description: Array of manual positions (custom billing items). These are manually added items like third-party costs, flat fees, interim payments, or other custom charges. Can include project manual positions or custom positions added during invoice review.

supportContingentCharges.description: Array of support contingent charges. These are retainer plan charges for Support-type projects, based on allocated hours and pricing for billing periods. Each charge includes the contingent title, period (start/end dates), frequency, hours included, price, hourly rate, and transferable flag.

skippedTasks.description: Array of task IDs (UUIDs) that have been excluded from billing. These are tasks that would normally be billable but have been skipped (marked as "do not bill").

skippedExpressQuotationTasks.description: Array of express quotation task IDs (UUIDs) that have been excluded from billing. These are accepted express quotations that have been skipped and will not appear on the invoice.

billedFrom.description: Billed from date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). If provided, updates the start date that will appear on the final invoice document. This can differ from the actual service period start date (periodFrom). Omit this field to leave unchanged. Accepts date-only format (e.g., "2025-10-01") or ISO timestamp.

billedTo.description: Billed to date (ISO 8601 date string, YYYY-MM-DD format). If provided, updates the end date that will appear on the final invoice document. This can differ from the actual service period end date (periodTo). Omit this field to leave unchanged. Accepts date-only format (e.g., "2025-10-31") or ISO timestamp.

contactId.description: Contact ID (organization member UUID) who will receive the invoice when it is sent via email. If provided, updates the invoice recipient. Set to null to clear the contact and remove the recipient assignment. Omit this field to leave unchanged. The contact must belong to the project customer organization (validated automatically).

value.description: Billed time in hours. This is the amount of time that will be charged to the customer, which may differ from the actual time spent. Must be a non-negative number.

billedTime.description: Billed time in hours. This is the amount of time that will be charged to the customer, which may differ from the actual time spent. Can be null to clear the billed time. Must be a non-negative number if provided.

billedTitle.description: Custom billed title for the task. This title will appear on the invoice instead of the task title. Can be null to use the original task title. If provided, must be a non-empty string.
billedTitle.example: Integration of BIM tools with custom workflow adjustments

skipFromBilling.description: Whether to skip this task from billing. Set to true to exclude the task from the invoice, or false to include it again.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for the manual position. This ID is used to reference the position in update and delete operations.

title.description: Title or description of the manual position (e.g., "Workshop travel expenses", "Translation services")

price.description: Net price (before tax) for this manual position in the workspace currency

title.description: Title or description of the manual position

price.description: Net price (before tax) for this manual position in the workspace currency

items.description: Array of manual position IDs in the desired sort order. Only custom manual positions (type: manual) can be reordered.

Array of subscription billing IDs (billingId) in the desired sort order. The subscriptions will appear on the invoice in this order. All subscription billing IDs from the potential invoice should be included.

value.description: Quantity value for the subscription billing row. For per-unit pricing subscriptions, this sets how many units to bill. Set to null or omit to clear a custom quantity and revert to default. Must be a non-negative integer if provided.

workspace.description: Workspace-wide helpdesk settings including the global enabled flag and default task creation settings. These settings serve as the base configuration for all helpdesk operations in the workspace.

emailAccounts.description: List of all email accounts in the workspace with their helpdesk settings. Each account can have account-specific overrides that override workspace defaults. Accounts without specific settings will use workspace defaults.

defaultTaskPriority.description: Default task priority for all helpdesk tasks. This priority will be assigned to tasks created from emails unless overridden at the email account or organization level. Valid values: Urgent, High, Normal, Low.

defaultTaskTypeId.description: Default task type ID that will be assigned to all helpdesk tasks. This determines the category/type of tasks created from emails. Must be a valid task type ID from your workspace.

defaultTaskStatusId.description: Default task status ID for newly created helpdesk tasks. This determines the initial status of tasks created from emails. Must be a valid task status ID from your workspace.

defaultReplyBody.description:
HTML template for automatic replies sent to new email senders. This template is used when `sendDefaultReply` is enabled. Accepts markdown or HTML for complex formatting. In HTML you can use: ## Node Types and Marks ### Nodes **paragraph** Type: block Content: inline* Atts: - extraPlaceholder: null ```html

``` **heading** Type: block Content: inline* Atts: - level: 1 ```html

``` **bulletList** Type: block list Content: listItem+ Atts: none ```html
``` **hardBreak** Type: inline Atts: none ```html
``` **horizontalRule** Type: block Atts: none ```html
``` **orderedList** Type: block list Content: listItem+ Atts: - start: 1 - type: null ```html
``` **listItem** Content: (paragraph|list)* Atts: none ```html
  • ``` **blockquote** Type: block Content: block+ Atts: none ```html

    ``` ### Marks **link** Atts: - href: null - target: _blank - rel: noopener noreferrer nofollow - class: null ```html link text example ``` **bold** Atts: none ```html bold text example ``` **code** Atts: none ```html code text example ``` **italic** Atts: none ```html italic text example ``` **strike** Atts: none ```html strike text example ``` **underline** Atts: none ```html underline text example ``` ## Available Helpdesk Variables You can use the following variables in your helpdesk templates. Variables are represented as HTML spans: **taskNumber**: The generated task number for the helpdesk ticket **Example HTML**: `#12345` **Note:** The template supports variables that are automatically replaced with actual values (e.g., `taskNumber` becomes the actual task number).
defaultReplyBody.example: Thank you for contacting us. Your ticket number is #12345.

sendDefaultReply.description: Whether to automatically send replies to new email senders. When enabled, the system will send the `defaultReplyBody` template as a reply to initial emails from senders who haven't contacted helpdesk before. Replies are only sent for first-time senders, not for replies to existing conversations.

autoAssignUserId.description: Optional user ID to automatically assign all helpdesk tasks to. If provided, all tasks created from emails will be assigned to this user. If omitted or null, tasks will be created without automatic assignment. Must be a valid user ID from your workspace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the email account. Use this ID to reference the account in other API calls.

email.description: Email address of the account. This is the address that receives helpdesk emails.

displayName.description: Display name for the email account. This is the friendly name shown in the UI and in email headers.

helpdeskEnabled.description: Whether helpdesk is currently enabled for this email account. When enabled, emails sent to this account will be automatically processed and converted to tasks. When disabled, emails are ignored.

settings.description: Account-specific helpdesk settings that override workspace defaults. Only present when helpdesk is enabled for this account. Null fields inherit from workspace defaults. If helpdesk is disabled, this field will be undefined.

defaultTaskPriority.description: Task priority override for this email account. If provided, this priority will be used instead of the workspace default. If omitted or null, the account will inherit the workspace default priority. Valid values: Urgent, High, Normal, Low.

defaultTaskTypeId.description: Task type ID override for this email account. If provided, this task type will be used instead of the workspace default. If omitted or null, the account will inherit the workspace default task type. Must be a valid task type ID from your workspace.

defaultTaskStatusId.description: Task status ID override for this email account. If provided, this task status will be used instead of the workspace default. If omitted or null, the account will inherit the workspace default task status. Must be a valid task status ID from your workspace.

defaultReplyBody.description:
HTML template override for automatic replies sent from this email account. If provided, this template will be used instead of the workspace default. If omitted or null, the account will inherit the workspace default reply template. Accepts markdown or HTML for complex formatting. In HTML you can use: ## Node Types and Marks ### Nodes **paragraph** Type: block Content: inline* Atts: - extraPlaceholder: null ```html

``` **horizontalRule** Type: block Atts: none ```html

Variables are represented as HTML spans: **taskNumber**: The generated task number for the helpdesk ticket **Example HTML**: `#12345` **Note:** Supports the same variables as workspace templates (e.g., `taskNumber`).
defaultReplyBody.example: Thank you for contacting us. Your ticket number is #12345.

sendDefaultReply.description: Auto-reply behavior override for this email account. This field supports 3-state inheritance: `null` (inherit from workspace default), `true` (enable auto-reply for this account), `false` (disable auto-reply for this account). This allows fine-grained control over auto-reply behavior per account.

autoAssignUserId.description: Auto-assign user override for this email account. If provided, tasks created from emails to this account will be assigned to this user. If omitted or null, the account will inherit the workspace default assignment (or no assignment if workspace default is also null). Set to an empty string to remove the override and use workspace default. Must be a valid user ID from your workspace.

settings.description: Organization-level helpdesk settings. These settings allow organizations to override workspace defaults for their email routing. Organization settings have higher priority than workspace defaults and can apply to all emails or only emails from organization members.

emailAccounts.description: List of email accounts with organization-specific helpdesk settings. Each account shows organization-level overrides and includes `effectiveDefaults` which shows the final merged settings (organization + workspace defaults). Organization settings override workspace defaults when emails are processed from organization members (or all senders if mode='all').

mode.description: Organization mode determines when these settings apply. `onlyMembers`: Settings apply only when the email sender is a member of this organization. `all`: Settings apply to ALL emails sent to the account, regardless of sender (highest priority). When set to `all`, these settings override everything including workspace defaults and other organization settings.

defaultTaskPriority.description: Default task priority for helpdesk tasks created from emails that match this organization's settings. This overrides workspace defaults when organization settings apply. Valid values: Urgent, High, Normal, Low.

defaultTaskTypeId.description: Default task type ID for helpdesk tasks created from emails that match this organization's settings. This overrides workspace defaults when organization settings apply. Must be a valid task type ID from your workspace.

defaultTaskStatusId.description: Default task status ID for helpdesk tasks created from emails that match this organization's settings. This overrides workspace defaults when organization settings apply. Must be a valid task status ID from your workspace.

defaultReplyBody.description:
HTML template for automatic replies sent to email senders when organization settings apply. This overrides workspace defaults when organization settings are used. Accepts markdown or HTML for complex formatting. In HTML you can use: ## Node Types and Marks ### Nodes **paragraph** Type: block Content: inline* Atts: - extraPlaceholder: null ```html

sendDefaultReply.description: Whether to automatically send replies to new email senders when organization settings apply. When enabled, the system will send the `defaultReplyBody` template as a reply to initial emails from senders who match this organization's settings.

autoAssignUserId.description: Optional user ID to automatically assign helpdesk tasks to when organization settings apply. If provided, tasks created from emails that match this organization's settings will be assigned to this user. If omitted or null, tasks will use workspace default assignment or no assignment. Must be a valid user ID from your workspace.

logoUrl.description: URL to the workspace logo for default theme

logoDarkThemeUrl.description: URL to the workspace logo for dark theme

logoId.description: File ID for the workspace logo (default theme). Upload file via POST /workspace/upload first.

logoDarkThemeId.description: File ID for the workspace logo (dark theme). Upload file via POST /workspace/upload first.

id.description: Workspace ID

brandColor.description: Brand color in hex format (e.g., #FF5733). Used for workspace branding and internal project colors throughout the application.

defaultLanguage.description: Default language code for the workspace. Supported values: "en" (English) or "de" (German). This applies to new users; each user can override this in their profile.

countryOfOperation.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (2 uppercase letters) for the country where the company operates. Example: "US" for United States, "DE" for Germany.

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier for the workspace. Defines the timezone used for all time-related information. Example: "America/New_York", "Europe/Berlin", "Asia/Tokyo".

attendanceEnabled.description: Whether attendance tracking (clock in/out) is enabled for employees. When enabled, employees must actively clock in and out. When disabled, working hours are calculated automatically based on contract times.

timeColorBadPercent.description: Lower threshold percentage (1-100) for marking time tracking as "bad" or incomplete. Times below this percentage are marked red on the team calendar. Must be less than timeColorGoodPercent.

timeColorGoodPercent.description: Upper threshold percentage (1-100) for marking time tracking as "good" or complete. Times above this percentage are marked green on the team calendar. Must be greater than timeColorBadPercent.

defaultTimeFrom.description: Default work start time for employees. Used as a preset when employees clock in. ISO 8601 datetime format. Must be before defaultTimeTo if both are provided.

defaultTimeTo.description: Default work end time for employees. Used as a preset when employees clock out. ISO 8601 datetime format. Must be after defaultTimeFrom if both are provided.

timeTrackerAnnoyanceInMin.description: Time tracker reminder interval in minutes (5-120). After this many minutes of inactivity, the system reminds users to resume time tracking. Example: 15 means reminders every 15 minutes.

sprintPeriodDays.description: Sprint duration in days. Defines how long each sprint period lasts. Commonly used values: 7 (one week), 14 (two weeks), or 30 (one month).

sprintStartDay.description: Sprint start day of the week (1-7, where 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, ..., 7=Sunday). Defines when each sprint period begins.

sprintEndDay.description: Sprint end day of the week (1-7, where 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, ..., 7=Sunday). Defines when each sprint period ends. If not provided, calculated from sprintPeriodDays.

journalNotificationUserIds.description: Array of user IDs (UUIDs) who should receive notifications when journal entries are created or updated. Journal entries document events, notes, or observations for employees, projects, or organizations.

brandColor.description: Brand color in hex format (e.g., #FF5733). Used for workspace branding and internal project colors throughout the application.

defaultLanguage.description: Default language code for the workspace. Supported values: "en" (English) or "de" (German). This applies to new users; each user can override this in their profile.

countryOfOperation.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (2 uppercase letters) for the country where the company operates. Example: "US" for United States, "DE" for Germany.

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier for the workspace. Defines the timezone used for all time-related information. Example: "America/New_York", "Europe/Berlin", "Asia/Tokyo".

attendanceEnabled.description: Whether attendance tracking (clock in/out) is enabled for employees. When enabled, employees must actively clock in and out. When disabled, working hours are calculated automatically based on contract times.

timeColorBadPercent.description: Lower threshold percentage (1-100) for marking time tracking as "bad" or incomplete. Times below this percentage are marked red on the team calendar. Must be less than timeColorGoodPercent.

timeColorGoodPercent.description: Upper threshold percentage (1-100) for marking time tracking as "good" or complete. Times above this percentage are marked green on the team calendar. Must be greater than timeColorBadPercent.

defaultTimeFrom.description: Default work start time for employees. Used as a preset when employees clock in. ISO 8601 datetime format. Must be before defaultTimeTo if both are provided.

defaultTimeTo.description: Default work end time for employees. Used as a preset when employees clock out. ISO 8601 datetime format. Must be after defaultTimeFrom if both are provided.

timeTrackerAnnoyanceInMin.description: Time tracker reminder interval in minutes (5-120). After this many minutes of inactivity, the system reminds users to resume time tracking. Example: 15 means reminders every 15 minutes.

sprintPeriodDays.description: Sprint duration in days. Defines how long each sprint period lasts. Commonly used values: 7 (one week), 14 (two weeks), or 30 (one month).

sprintStartDay.description: Sprint start day of the week (1-7, where 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, ..., 7=Sunday). Defines when each sprint period begins.

sprintEndDay.description: Sprint end day of the week (1-7, where 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, ..., 7=Sunday). Defines when each sprint period ends. If not provided, calculated from sprintPeriodDays.

journalNotificationUserIds.description: Array of user IDs (UUIDs) who should receive notifications when journal entries are created or updated. Journal entries document events, notes, or observations for employees, projects, or organizations.

code.description: Country or region code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. For states/provinces, uses format "COUNTRY-STATE" (e.g., "US-CA" for California).

name.description: Localized country or region name in the requested language. Names are provided in the language specified by the `language` query parameter.

defaultHolidayCode.description: The default holiday country/region code for the workspace. This code is automatically applied to all new employees. Individual employees can override this in their profile settings.

availableCountries.description: Complete list of all available holiday countries and states that can be used for holiday configurations. Use these codes when setting default holiday settings or individual employee holiday rules.

defaultHolidayCode.description: The default holiday country/region code to set for the workspace. Must be one of the available codes from `GET /holidays/countries`. Examples: "US" (United States), "DE" (Germany), "US-CA" (California), "AT" (Austria). This setting only affects new employees - existing employees keep their current holiday settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the holiday day. Only present for holidays in custom or derived years.

name.description: The name of the holiday (e.g., "Christmas Day", "New Year's Day", "Company Outing").

date.description: The computed date of the holiday in ISO 8601 format. For Fixed holidays, this is the specific date. For Rule-based holidays, this is the date computed from the rule for the given year.

type.description: The type of holiday: "Fixed" for specific dates, "Rule" for computed dates based on rules.

rule.description: Rule string for rule-based holidays (only present for Rule type). Examples: "25 december", "easter monday", "first monday in september". The date is automatically computed from this rule for each year.

substitute.description: Whether this is a substitute holiday (a replacement for a holiday that falls on a weekend). Substitute holidays are automatically created when official holidays fall on non-working days.

id.description: Unique identifier for the holiday year. Only present for custom or derived years. Default years do not have an ID as they cannot be directly modified.

year.description: The year this holiday configuration applies to.

country.description: Country or region code for this holiday configuration (e.g., "US", "DE", "US-CA").

type.description:
The type of holiday configuration:
- `"default"`: Official holidays from the holiday data source (cannot be modified)
- `"custom"`: Workspace-specific configuration (can be modified)
- `"derived"`: Configuration inherited from a previous custom year (can be modified)

derivedFrom.description: The year this configuration was derived from. Only present for derived type configurations. Indicates which custom year this configuration was copied from.

validTill.description: The last year this configuration applies to. If `null`, the configuration applies indefinitely to all future years. Only present for custom or derived years. Controls how the configuration propagates to future years.

holidays.description: Complete list of all holidays for this year, including both fixed and rule-based holidays. Holidays are sorted by date and include computed dates for rule-based holidays.

year.description: The year to create a custom holiday configuration for. Must be 1900 or later. If a custom year already exists for this year/country combination, it will be deleted and recreated with default holidays.

country.description: Country or region code (e.g., "US", "DE", "US-CA"). Must be one of the available codes from `GET /holidays/countries`.

validTill.description: The last year this configuration applies to. If `null`, the configuration applies indefinitely to all future years. If set to a specific year (e.g., 2026), the configuration applies from the specified year through that year. Future years within this range will automatically be created as "derived" configurations. You can update this later using `PUT /holidays/years/:yearId`.

The last year this configuration applies to. If `null`, the configuration applies indefinitely to all future years. If set to a specific year (e.g., 2026), the configuration applies from the year's start year through that year. Future years within this range will automatically be created as "derived" configurations. Years beyond `validTill` will use default holidays.

yearId.description: The UUID of the custom holiday year this holiday belongs to. Must be a custom year (created via `POST /holidays/years`). You cannot add holidays to default years.

name.description: The name of the holiday. Should be descriptive and clear (e.g., "Company Outing", "Festivus", "Team Building Day").

type.description: Holiday type. Only "Fixed" is allowed when creating new holidays. Rule-based holidays can only be edited, not created, as they come from default holiday definitions.

date.description: The specific date for the holiday in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2024-12-25" or "2024-06-15"). The date should be in the format YYYY-MM-DD. The time portion is ignored.

id.description: Unique identifier for the holiday day.

yearId.description: The UUID of the holiday year this holiday belongs to.

name.description: The name of the holiday.

type.description: The type of holiday: "Fixed" for specific dates, "Rule" for computed dates based on rules.

date.description: The date of the holiday in ISO 8601 format. Present for Fixed holidays. For Rule-based holidays, this is the computed date based on the rule for the year.

rule.description: Rule string for rule-based holidays. Only present when `type` is "Rule". The date is automatically computed from this rule for each year.

name.description: The name of the holiday. Can be changed to rename the holiday (e.g., "Christmas" → "Holiday Break").

type.description: The type of holiday: "Fixed" for specific dates, "Rule" for computed dates based on rules. You can convert between types, but converting from Fixed to Rule requires providing a `rule` field.

date.description: The specific date for fixed holidays in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2024-12-25"). Required when `type` is "Fixed". The date should be in the format YYYY-MM-DD. The time portion is ignored.

rule.description: Rule string for rule-based holidays (only for Rule type). Examples: "25 december", "easter monday", "first monday in september". If not provided when switching to Rule type, the existing rule will be preserved (if available). If converting from Fixed to Rule without an existing rule, you must provide this field.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the custom icon

name.description: Name of the custom icon (e.g., `:company_logo:`, `:team_sales:`). This is the identifier used to reference the icon throughout the workspace.

imageId.description: File ID (UUID) of the icon image file

imageUrl.description: Full public URL to access and display the icon image. Use this URL to display the icon in your application.

authorId.description: ID (UUID) of the user who created this custom icon

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 date and time when the custom icon was created

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 date and time when the custom icon was last updated (image replacement)

name.description: Unique name for the custom icon. Must be alphanumeric characters and underscores only. Cannot conflict with default emoji names. Typically formatted with colons (e.g., `:company_logo:`, `:team_sales:`, `:lt_editor:`). This name is used to reference the icon throughout the workspace.

imageId.description: File ID (UUID) of the uploaded icon image. First upload the image file (PNG or SVG format) using POST /api/public/workspace/upload, then use the returned file ID here.

File ID (UUID) of the new icon image. First upload the new image file (PNG or SVG format) using POST /api/public/workspace/upload, then use the returned file ID here. Note: The icon name cannot be changed after creation - only the image can be updated.

items.description: Array of time booking entries matching the filter criteria. Each item represents a single time log entry with details about when, who, what, and how much time was logged.

id.description: Unique identifier of the time booking entry

taskId.description: UUID of the task this time was logged to. Null if time was logged directly to a project.

projectId.description: UUID of the project this time was logged to. Always present.

userId.description: UUID of the user (employee) who logged this time

date.description: Date when the work was performed (ISO 8601 format)

hours.description: Number of hours logged (decimal format, e.g., 8.5 for 8 hours 30 minutes)

comment.description: Optional comment describing the work performed

activityId.description: UUID of the activity type (e.g., Development, Design, Management)

total.description: Total number of time booking entries matching the filter criteria across all pages. Use this to calculate total pages: Math.ceil(total / pageSize)

totalHours.description: Sum of all hours across all filtered time booking entries. This is useful for reporting and calculating total time spent. The value is the sum of the hours field from all matching entries.

page.description: Current page number (1-based). The first page is page 1.

pageSize.description: Number of items per page. This is the page size you requested (or the default if not specified). The actual number of items in the items array may be less than this if you are on the last page.

totalChildren.description: Total number of child items when grouping is enabled. With strict raw-record pagination, this equals total. Only present when grouping is used.

groupMeta.description: Group metadata when grouping is enabled (e.g. group=day, group=week, group=month). Each entry describes a group on the current page: groupKey, pageFragmentCount, fullGroupCount, pageFragmentSum, fullGroupSum, and continuation flags. Order matches group appearance in items.

type.description: Type of time booking. Use "task" to log time to a specific task, or "project" to log time directly to a project. Task-based bookings are linked to both the task and its parent project. Project-based bookings are only linked to the project.

id.description: UUID of the task to log time to. Required when type is "task". The task must exist in your workspace and be linked to a project.

projectId.description: UUID of the project to log time to. Required when type is "project". The project must exist in your workspace.

activityId.description: UUID of the activity type that describes the work performed. Activities are defined in your workspace settings (e.g., "Development", "Design", "Management", "Research"). Activities help categorize time for reporting and analysis.

hours.description: Number of hours logged. Must be a positive number. Can be a decimal (e.g., 2.5 for 2 hours 30 minutes). If you also provide minutes, they will be added to this value. Required if minutes is not provided or is 0.

minutes.description: Additional minutes to add to the hours. Must be between 0 and 59. These minutes are converted and added to the hours value (e.g., hours: 2, minutes: 30 = 2.5 hours total). Optional - if not provided, defaults to 0.

comment.description: Optional comment describing what was accomplished during this time. Useful for documentation and later reference. Can be searched in the time tracking grid.

date.description: Date when the work was performed. Must be in ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). This is the date the time is attributed to, not necessarily when the booking was created. You can log time retroactively for past dates.

type.description: Type of time booking. Change to "task" to link to a specific task, or "project" for project-only booking. If changing type, make sure to provide the corresponding ID field (id for task, projectId for project). If omitted, the existing type is preserved.

id.description: UUID of the task to link this time booking to. Required if type is "task". Use this to move time from one task to another, or to change from project-based to task-based booking. If omitted and type is not changed, the existing task link is preserved.

projectId.description: UUID of the project to link this time booking to. Required if type is "project". Use this to move time from one project to another, or to change from task-based to project-based booking. If omitted and type is not changed, the existing project link is preserved.

activityId.description: UUID of the activity type. Change this to recategorize the work type. If omitted, the existing activity is preserved.

hours.description: Number of hours to update. Must be a positive number. Can be a decimal (e.g., 3.5 for 3 hours 30 minutes). If you also provide minutes, they will be added to this value. If omitted, the existing hours value is preserved.

minutes.description: Additional minutes to add to the hours. Must be between 0 and 59. These minutes are converted and added to the hours value. If omitted, defaults to 0 (only the hours value is used).

comment.description: Comment text. Update this to change or add notes about the work performed. If omitted, the existing comment is preserved. To clear a comment, provide an empty string.

date.description: Date when the work was performed. Must be in ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). Use this to correct the date if the time was logged to the wrong day. If omitted, the existing date is preserved.

start.description: Interval start time in ISO 8601 format (UTC). This is when the time tracking period began.

end.description: Interval end time in ISO 8601 format (UTC). For active (running) intervals, this is set to the current time. For completed intervals, this marks when tracking stopped. Intervals that span midnight are automatically ended at end of day.

id.description: Unique identifier for the time tracker (UUID)

timeTrackingType.description: Type of time tracking: Task (tracking time on a specific task) or Project (tracking time at project level). Determines whether taskId is required.

projectId.description: UUID of the project this tracker is associated with. Can be null if tracking general work.

taskId.description: UUID of the task this tracker is associated with. Only used when timeTrackingType is Task. Can be null for project-level tracking.

activityId.description: UUID of the activity category (e.g., Development, Testing, Management). Used for categorizing work in reports. Can be null.

comment.description: Optional comment or description of the work being tracked. Helps provide context when reviewing time entries later.

correction.description: Manual time adjustment in minutes. Positive values add time, negative values subtract time. Useful for correcting tracking errors or adding time that was not tracked. Default is 0.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tracker was created, in ISO 8601 format (UTC)

intervals.description: Array of time intervals that make up the total tracked time. Each interval represents a continuous period of tracking (start to end). If an interval has no end, it is currently active. Intervals are automatically ended at end of day if they span midnight.

isRunning.description: Whether the tracker currently has an active (running) interval. True if any interval has no end time, meaning time is being tracked right now.

hours.description: Total tracked time in hours, calculated from all intervals plus any correction. Minimum value is 0.0166667 (1 minute).

minutesPadded.description: Minutes portion of the tracked time, formatted as a zero-padded string (e.g., "05", "30", "59")

hoursPadded.description: Hours portion of the tracked time, formatted as a zero-padded string (e.g., "02", "12", "00")

autoEnded.description: Whether any interval was automatically ended at the end of the day. This happens when a running interval started on a previous day and was not manually stopped.

isOld.description: Whether the tracker contains intervals from previous days. True if any interval start time is not from today.

trackers.description: Array of all time trackers belonging to the authenticated user, sorted by creation time. Each tracker includes calculated fields like isRunning, hours, and formatted time strings.

lastInteraction.description: Timestamp of the last interaction with any tracker (create, update, pause, resume, etc.) in ISO 8601 format (UTC). Used to track user activity. Can be null if no trackers exist.

timeTrackingType.description: Required. Type of time tracking: Task (for tracking time on a specific task) or Project (for tracking time at project level). Determines whether taskId should be provided.

projectId.description: Optional. UUID of the project to associate with this tracker. If provided, the tracked time will be linked to this project.

taskId.description: Optional. UUID of the task to associate with this tracker. Only used when timeTrackingType is Task. If timeTrackingType is Task, you should typically provide a taskId.

activityId.description: Optional. UUID of the activity category (e.g., Development, Testing, Management). Used to categorize the type of work being tracked for reporting purposes.

comment.description: Optional. Free-text comment describing the work being tracked. Helps provide context when reviewing time entries later.

correction.description: Optional. Manual time adjustment in minutes. Positive values add time (e.g., if you started tracking late), negative values subtract time. Default is 0.

timeTrackingType.description: Optional. Change the time tracking type between Task and Project. If changed, ensure taskId is appropriate for the new type.

projectId.description: Optional. Update or remove the project association. Provide a UUID to change the project, or null to remove the association. Only provided fields are updated.

taskId.description: Optional. Update or remove the task association. Provide a UUID to change the task, or null to remove the association. Only used when timeTrackingType is Task.

activityId.description: Optional. Update or remove the activity category. Provide a UUID to change the activity, or null to remove it.

comment.description: Optional. Update or remove the comment. Provide a string to update the comment, or null to remove it.

correction.description: Optional. Adjust the tracked time by providing a correction in minutes. Positive values add time, negative values subtract time. This affects the total hours calculation.

companyName.description: Official business name of the company. Used in documents, invoices, contracts, and projects throughout the system.

companyLegalForm.description: Legal form or structure of the company (e.g., GmbH, AG, LLC, Inc., sole proprietorship). Used in legal documents and official communications.

companyStreet.description: Street name of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyZip.description: ZIP or postal code of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyCity.description: City of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyCountry.description: Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, e.g., "DE", "US") of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyHouseNumber.description: House number of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyPhone.description: Official company phone number. Used for contact information in documents and communications.

companyEmail.description: Main business contact email address. Used for contact information in documents and communications.

companyWebsite.description: Company website URL (optional). Used for contact information in documents and communications.

companyFax.description: Company fax number (optional). Used for contact information in documents and communications. For companies that still use fax.

companyTaxNumber.description: Tax identification number. Required for invoices and tax documents. Format varies by country (e.g., "DE123456789" for Germany).

companyRegistrationNumber.description: Business registration number or commercial registry entry number. Format varies by country (e.g., "HRB 12345" for German companies).

companyRegistrationCourt.description: Responsible registration court where the company is registered. Example: "Amtsgericht Berlin" for German companies.

companyShortDescription.description: Short description of what the company does. Optional field to describe the company's business activities. Example: "Manufacturer of Leadtime – an ERP system for software providers."

invoiceAccountNumber.description: Bank account number in IBAN format used for invoices. This account number appears on invoices for payment purposes.

companyName.description: Official business name of the company. Used in documents, invoices, contracts, and projects throughout the system.

companyLegalForm.description: Legal form or structure of the company (e.g., GmbH, AG, LLC, Inc., sole proprietorship). Used in legal documents and official communications.

companyStreet.description: Street name of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyZip.description: ZIP or postal code of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyCity.description: City of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyCountry.description: Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, e.g., "DE", "US") of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyHouseNumber.description: House number of the company address. Part of the complete address used on invoices, contracts, and other documents.

companyPhone.description: Official company phone number. Used for contact information in documents and communications.

companyEmail.description: Main business contact email address. Used for contact information in documents and communications. Must be valid email format.

companyWebsite.description: Company website URL (optional). Used for contact information in documents and communications. Must be valid URL format.

companyFax.description: Company fax number (optional). Used for contact information in documents and communications. For companies that still use fax.

companyTaxNumber.description: Tax identification number. Required for invoices and tax documents. Format varies by country (e.g., "DE123456789" for Germany).

companyRegistrationNumber.description: Business registration number or commercial registry entry number. Format varies by country (e.g., "HRB 12345" for German companies).

companyRegistrationCourt.description: Responsible registration court where the company is registered. Example: "Amtsgericht Berlin" for German companies.

companyShortDescription.description: Short description of what the company does. Optional field to describe the company's business activities. Example: "Manufacturer of Leadtime – an ERP system for software providers."

invoiceAccountNumber.description: Bank account number in IBAN format used for invoices. This account number appears on invoices for payment purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for this journal entry

memberId.description: UUID of the organization member (external contact) this journal entry is associated with. This identifies which external contact the entry is about.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when this journal entry was created. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ (e.g., "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z").

createdBy.description: UUID of the user who created this journal entry. This identifies who wrote the entry, which is useful for tracking authorship and accountability.

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when this journal entry was last modified. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ (e.g., "2024-01-16T14:20:00Z"). This is automatically updated whenever the entry is edited via the PATCH endpoint.

mood.description: Emotional indicator for the journal entry. Values: `Happy` (positive interaction), `Neutral` (standard/neutral interaction), or `Sad` (negative or concerning interaction). This helps categorize and filter entries by emotional context.

reminder.description: Optional reminder date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Set when creating or updating the entry to track follow-up dates for goals, agreements, or actions. Returns `null` if no reminder is set. Example: "2024-12-31" for December 31, 2024.

body.description: Journal entry content in HTML format. The content is automatically converted from the internal document format (IDoc) to HTML for easy display in web applications. Contains the rich text content including headings, paragraphs, lists, links, and formatting as originally provided.
body.example:
Member Update

Had a productive meeting today. Discussed project timeline and next steps.

memberId.description: UUID of the organization member (external contact) this journal entry is about. The member must exist in the workspace and be accessible to the current user. Organization members can only create entries for their own member record unless they have the `Organizations.writeJournal` permission.

Member Update

Had a productive meeting today. Discussed project timeline and next steps.

mood.description: Emotional indicator for the journal entry. Use this to quickly categorize the tone or sentiment of the interaction. Options: `Happy` (positive interaction), `Neutral` (standard/neutral interaction), or `Sad` (negative or concerning interaction). Defaults to `Neutral` if not provided. This helps filter and analyze journal entries by emotional context.

reminder.description: Optional reminder date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Use this to set a follow-up date for reviewing goals, agreements, or taking action related to this journal entry. The reminder date can be used to filter entries and trigger notifications. Example: "2024-12-31" for December 31, 2024. If not provided, no reminder is set.

memberId.description: UUID of the organization member to associate this journal entry with. If provided, changes which member this entry is about. The new member must exist and be accessible. If the new member is the current user's own member record, `visibleToSubject` will be automatically set to `true`. If omitted, the existing member association is preserved.

Updated Member Status

mood.description: Updated emotional indicator for the journal entry. Options: `Happy` (positive interaction), `Neutral` (standard/neutral interaction), or `Sad` (negative or concerning interaction). If omitted, the existing mood value remains unchanged.

id.description: Organization member ID

roleId.description: User role ID that determines the member's access permissions in Leadtime. Can be `null` if the member does not have login access.

canLogin.description: Whether member can login to the system

title.description: Title/salutation

birthDate.description: Date of birth in ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). Returns `null` if not set.

avatarUrl.description: Public URL to the member's avatar/profile picture. Returns `null` if no avatar has been uploaded. The URL can be used directly in image tags or for display purposes.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the organization member was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the organization member was last updated.

organizationId.description: UUID of the organization this member belongs to. The organization must exist in your workspace.

email.description: Email address of the member. This email will be used for the associated user account and for sending invitation emails if login access is enabled. Must be a valid email format.

roleId.description: User role ID that determines the member's access permissions in Leadtime. Common roles include guest roles (e.g., `workspace-id_guest_`) for external users. This role is applied to the automatically created user account.

isActive.description: Whether the member is active in the system. Inactive members are hidden from normal views but remain in the database. Defaults to `true`.

canLogin.description: Whether the member can log in to Leadtime as a guest user. If `true`, a user account is created with Active status and an invitation email is sent. If `false` or omitted, the member exists as a contact only with an Inactive user account. Defaults to `false`.

title.description: Title/salutation (e.g., Dr., Prof.)

position.description: Job title or position within the organization (e.g., "Managing Director", "Project Manager", "Head of Sales").

avatarId.description: Avatar file ID (upload via POST /workspace/upload first)

firstName.description: First name. If provided, this change will automatically sync to the associated user account.

lastName.description: Last name. If provided, this change will automatically sync to the associated user account.

email.description: Email address. If provided, this change will automatically sync to the associated user account. Must be a valid email format.

roleId.description: User role ID that determines access permissions. If provided, this change will automatically sync to the associated user account, updating their permissions in Leadtime.

isActive.description: Whether the member is active in the system. Inactive members are hidden from normal views but remain in the database.

canLogin.description: Whether the member can log in to Leadtime. Changing this value will update the associated user account: setting to `true` activates the user and sends an invitation email; setting to `false` deactivates the user and clears the invite token.

gender.description: Gender of the member. Used for personalized greetings in letters and communications. Available values: `Male`, `Female`.

title.description: Title or salutation prefix (e.g., "Dr.", "Prof.", "Mr.", "Mrs."). Used in formal communications and letters.

degree.description: Academic degree or qualification (e.g., "Ph.D.", "MBA", "M.Sc."). Used in formal communications.

position.description: Job title or position within the organization (e.g., "Managing Director", "Project Manager", "Head of Sales").

addressStreet.description: Street name for the member's address. Part of the complete address information.

addressZip.description: ZIP or postal code for the member's address. Part of the complete address information.

addressCity.description: City for the member's address. Part of the complete address information.

addressCountry.description: Country for the member's address. Part of the complete address information.

addressHouseNumber.description: House or building number for the member's address. Part of the complete address information.

phone.description: Phone number for contacting the member. Can include country code prefix (e.g., +1 for US).

birthDate.description: Date of birth in ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). Used for birthday reminders and age calculations.

socialNetworkLink.description: URL to a social network profile (e.g., LinkedIn, Twitter, XING). Used for relationship management and contact information.

influenceLevel.description: Classification of the member's level of influence within their organization. Used for relationship management and strategic planning. Available values: `Low`, `Medium`, `High`, `Dominant`.

attitudeLevel.description: Classification of the member's attitude towards your company. Used for relationship management and communication strategy. Available values: `Enthusiastic`, `WellMeaning`, `Neutral`, `Opposing`.

personalityType.description: Personality type classification based on behavioral patterns. Used for tailoring communication and interaction approaches. Available values: `Dominant`, `Conscientious`, `Steady`, `Initiative`.

avatarId.description: UUID of the avatar/profile picture file. To update: upload via `POST /workspace/upload` first, then provide the file ID. To remove: set to `null` or empty string. If not provided, the avatar remains unchanged.

OrgLetterAdressType.description: The address type used for this letter. "Organization" means the organization's main address was used, "Member" means a member's private address was used.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the organization letter

organizationId.description: UUID of the organization this letter belongs to. Use this to link the letter to the correct organization.

memberId.description: UUID of the organization member (contact person) this letter is addressed to. Will be null if the letter is addressed to the organization itself rather than a specific member.

subject.description: The subject or title of the letter as displayed in listings and on the document.

dateOfLetter.description: The date shown on the letter in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ). This is the date that appears on the letter document itself, which may differ from the creation date.

addressType.description: The address type used for this letter. "Organization" means the organization's main address was used, "Member" means a member's private address was used.

body.description: The letter body content in HTML format. This has been converted from the internal IDoc storage format to HTML for easy display and processing. Includes all formatting, template variables (as HTML spans), and rich text elements.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ) indicating when the letter was first created in the system.

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ) indicating when the letter was last modified. This is automatically updated whenever any field of the letter is changed.

createdBy.description: UUID of the user who created this letter. Use this to track authorship and for audit purposes.

organizationId.description: The UUID of the organization this letter belongs to. The organization must exist in your workspace and be accessible with your permissions.

memberId.description: Optional UUID of the organization member (contact person) this letter is addressed to. If provided, the member must belong to the specified organization. Leave empty or set to null for letters addressed to the organization itself.

subject.description: The subject or title of the letter. This appears in letter listings and as the document title when exporting to PDF.

dateOfLetter.description: The date shown on the letter (ISO 8601 date format: YYYY-MM-DD). This can be backdated if needed. The date is displayed on the letter document and used for sorting and filtering.

addressType.description: Determines which address is used on the letter. Use "Organization" for the organization's main address, or "Member" for the member's private address (requires memberId to be set).

``` **heading** Type: block Content: inline* Atts: - level: 1 ```html

``` **bulletList** Type: block list Content: listItem+ Atts: none ```html
``` **hardBreak** Type: inline Atts: none ```html

    ``` **variable** Type: inline Atts: - id: null ```html ``` ### Marks **link** Atts: - href: null - target: _blank - rel: noopener noreferrer nofollow - class: null ```html link text example ``` **bold** Atts: none ```html bold text example ``` **code** Atts: none ```html code text example ``` **italic** Atts: none ```html italic text example ``` **strike** Atts: none ```html strike text example ``` **underline** Atts: none ```html underline text example ``` ## Available Template Variables You can use the following variables in your organization letter body. Variables are represented as HTML spans: `clientCompanyName`, `contactPerson`, `senderNameAndPosition`, `senderName`, `senderPosition`, `todayDate`, `dearCustomer`, `signature` **Example HTML**: `ACME Corp`, `John Doe`
body.example:
Dear Customer

This is a reminder for invoice #12345...

organizationId.description: Optional: The UUID of the organization this letter belongs to. If provided, changes which organization the letter is linked to. The organization must exist and be accessible in your workspace.

memberId.description: Optional: The UUID of the organization member this letter is addressed to. If provided, links the letter to that member (member must belong to the specified or current organization). Set to null to clear the member association. Omit this field to leave it unchanged.

subject.description: Optional: The subject or title of the letter. If provided, updates the letter subject. Omit to leave unchanged.

dateOfLetter.description: Optional: The date shown on the letter (ISO 8601 date format: YYYY-MM-DD). If provided, updates the letter date. Omit to leave unchanged.

addressType.description: Optional: The address type for the letter. Use "Organization" for the organization's main address, or "Member" for the member's private address. If provided, updates the address type. Omit to leave unchanged.

Variables are represented as HTML spans: `clientCompanyName`, `contactPerson`, `senderNameAndPosition`, `senderName`, `senderPosition`, `todayDate`, `dearCustomer`, `signature` **Example HTML**: `ACME Corp`, `John Doe`

language.description: ISO 639-1 language code for the translation (e.g., "en" for English, "de" for German)

name.description: Translated name of the custom field in this language. Used for multilingual display in organization forms.

description.description: Translated description of the custom field in this language. Provides context about what information should be entered.

language.description: ISO 639-1 language code for the translation (e.g., "en" for English, "de" for German)

name.description: Translated display name for the select option in this language. Shown to users when selecting from dropdown options.

id.description: Optional unique identifier for the select option. If not provided, the value will be used as the identifier. Required when updating existing options.

value.description: Display value for the select option. This is the value stored when the option is selected. Must be unique within the field.

translations.description: Array of translations for multilingual support. Each translation contains a language code and translated display name. The system automatically shows the correct language based on user preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for the custom field. Used to reference this field when setting values on organizations.

name.description: Display name of the custom field. Shown as the field label in organization forms.

description.description: Description providing context about what information should be entered in this field.

sort.description: Numeric sort order for display purposes. Lower numbers appear first in organization forms. Determines the order in which fields are displayed.

type.description: Field type enum value indicating the input type. Valid values: Text, Textarea, Number, Date, Checkbox, Select, MultiSelect.

entity.description: Entity type identifier. Always "Organization" for organization custom fields. Used internally to scope fields to specific entity types.

translations.description: Complete array of translations for the field name. Each translation contains the language code and localized name.

selectOptions.description: Array of select options for Select and MultiSelect field types. Each option contains an ID, value, and translations. Empty array for other field types.

id.description: Optional unique identifier for the custom field. Only needed when updating an existing field. If omitted, a new field will be created.

name.description: Display name for the custom field. Shown as the field label in organization forms (e.g., "Priority Level", "Region", "Industry").

description.description: Optional description providing context about what information should be entered in this field. Helps users understand the purpose of the field.

type.description: Field type enum value that determines what kind of input is used. Valid values: Text (single-line), Textarea (multi-line), Number, Date, Checkbox, Select (single choice), MultiSelect (multiple choices).

selectOptions.description: Array of select options. REQUIRED for Select and MultiSelect field types. Each option contains a value and translations. Not used for other field types.

translations.description: Array of translations for the field name. Each translation contains a language code and translated name. The system automatically displays the correct language based on user preferences.

translationsDescriptions.description: Optional array of translations for the field description. Each translation contains a language code and translated description. Used for multilingual help text.

type.description: Type of organization defining the business relationship. color.description: Hex color code used to visually distinguish this organization in lists, projects, and throughout the application (required)

startOfCollaboration.description: Date when the business relationship with this organization started. Format: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD).

hourRate.description: Customer-specific hourly rate used for all billable time entries for this organization. Overrides workspace default. Used when creating invoices.

invoiceDueDays.description: Number of days after invoice date until payment is due. Valid range: 1-120. Overrides workspace default for this organization.

enableInvoiceReminderFee.description: Whether to charge a reminder fee for overdue invoices. Overrides workspace default. Set to true to enable, false to disable.

invoiceReminderFee.description: Amount charged as a reminder fee for overdue invoices. Valid range: 0-1000. Only applies if enableInvoiceReminderFee is true.

enableInvoiceInterest.description: Whether to charge interest on overdue invoices. Overrides workspace default. Set to true to enable, false to disable.

baseInvoiceInterest.description: Base interest rate percentage (0-1000) used as the foundation for calculating default interest. Typically set by central banks (e.g., ECB, Bundesbank) and changes every six months. The final interest rate is baseInvoiceInterest + invoiceInterest.

invoiceInterest.description: Interest rate percentage (0-1000) added to baseInvoiceInterest to calculate the total default interest rate. According to BGB §288: typically +5% for private customers, +9% for businesses. Final rate = baseInvoiceInterest + invoiceInterest.

invoiceLanguage.description: Language code used for generating invoices and reminders for this organization (e.g., "en", "de", "fr"). Overrides workspace default.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user (UUID format). Use this ID when assigning users to teams or referencing team members.

firstName.description: The user first name as stored in their employee profile.

lastName.description: The user last name as stored in their employee profile.

avatarId.description: Unique identifier for the user avatar image file (UUID format). This ID can be used to retrieve the avatar image through the file API. Null if the user has not uploaded an avatar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the team (UUID format). Use this ID to reference the team in update, delete, or project assignment operations.

name.description: The display name of the team. This name appears in team lists, project assignments, and throughout the application.

icon.description: Emoji icon used for visual identification of the team. This icon appears next to the team name in lists and can be used to quickly identify teams. Common examples include: "🧑‍🤝‍🧑" (people holding hands), "💻" (laptop), "🎨" (artist palette).

usersCount.description: Total number of users currently assigned to the team. This count matches the length of the users array and is provided for convenience.

users.description: Complete list of all users who are members of this team. Each user object includes their ID, name, and avatar information. Use the user IDs from this array when you need to reference team members.

name.description: The display name for the team. Choose a descriptive name that clearly identifies the team purpose or department. Examples: "Development Team", "Marketing", "Customer Support", "Sales Team". This name will appear in team lists and project assignments.

icon.description: Emoji icon for visual identification of the team. This icon appears next to the team name throughout the application. Choose any emoji that represents the team (e.g., "🧑‍🤝‍🧑" for teams, "💻" for development, "🎨" for design). The icon must be a non-empty string.

users.description: Array of user IDs (UUIDs) to assign as initial team members. At least one user must be provided. All user IDs must be valid UUIDs of existing employees in the workspace. You can add or remove members later using the update endpoint.

name.description: New display name for the team. If provided, replaces the existing team name. If omitted, the team name remains unchanged.

icon.description: New emoji icon for the team. If provided, replaces the existing team icon. If omitted, the team icon remains unchanged.

users.description: Complete array of user IDs (UUIDs) that should be members of the team. If provided, this array replaces the entire team membership (it is not merged with existing members). You must include all users you want in the team, including existing members you want to keep. At least one user must be included. If omitted, the team membership remains unchanged. All user IDs must be valid UUIDs of existing employees in the workspace.

reason.description: Optional reason explaining why the vacation request was declined or cancelled. This reason will be stored with the vacation record and visible in the vacation history. Providing a reason helps employees understand the decision and improves transparency in the approval process.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for this journal entry

employeeId.description: UUID of the employee this journal entry is associated with. This identifies which employee the entry is about.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when this journal entry was created. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ (e.g., "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z").

createdBy.description: UUID of the user who created this journal entry. This identifies who wrote the entry, which is useful for tracking authorship and accountability.

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when this journal entry was last modified. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ (e.g., "2024-01-16T14:20:00Z"). This is automatically updated whenever the entry is edited via the PATCH endpoint.

mood.description: Emotional indicator for the journal entry. Values: `Happy` (positive interaction), `Neutral` (standard/neutral interaction), or `Sad` (negative or concerning interaction). This helps categorize and filter entries by emotional context.

reminder.description: Optional reminder date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Set when creating or updating the entry to track follow-up dates for goals, agreements, or actions. Returns `null` if no reminder is set. Example: "2024-12-31" for December 31, 2024.

body.description: Journal entry content in HTML format. The content is automatically converted from the internal document format (IDoc) to HTML for easy display in web applications. Contains the rich text content including headings, paragraphs, lists, links, and formatting as originally provided.
body.example:
Employee Update

Had a productive meeting today. Discussed project timeline and next steps.

Employee Update

Had a productive meeting today. Discussed project timeline and next steps.

mood.description: Emotional indicator for the journal entry. Use this to quickly categorize the tone or sentiment of the interaction. Options: `Happy` (positive interaction), `Neutral` (standard/neutral interaction), or `Sad` (negative or concerning interaction). Defaults to `Neutral` if not provided. This helps filter and analyze journal entries by emotional context.

reminder.description: Optional reminder date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Use this to set a follow-up date for reviewing goals, agreements, or taking action related to this journal entry. The reminder date can be used to filter entries and trigger notifications. Example: "2024-12-31" for December 31, 2024. If not provided, no reminder is set.

employeeId.description: UUID of the employee to associate this journal entry with. If provided, changes which employee this entry is about. The new employee must exist and be accessible. If the new employee is the current user's own employee record, `visibleToSubject` will be automatically set to `true`. If omitted, the existing employee association is preserved.
